prompt: Photorealistic scene of an old wooden workshop bench with natural daylight from a dusty window. In the foreground, a highly detailed, overly intricate wooden key lies next to a single weathered wooden door lock. The key is carved with extreme precision, perfectly matching every tiny notch, groove, splinter, and imperfection of that exact keyhole — it fits flawlessly. Scattered around the bench are 8-10 other slightly different wooden keyholes and locks of varying shapes, none of which this elaborate key could possibly open. The key is beautifully but excessively complex, with unnecessary fine details and flourishes. Realistic wood textures, dust particles in light beams, tools like chisels and files in the background, warm natural lighting, shallow depth of field, cinematic and tangible like a National Geographic photo. No text, no labels, no diagrams, no mathematical symbols, physically plausible real-world scene only.
metaphor: An overly intricate key perfectly carved to match one specific keyhole but useless for any other locks — memorizing training data noise without generalizing.
